tool.name: FiLT3r
tool.description: alignment-free detection of internal (tandem) duplications from raw sequencing data
tool.command: filt3r -k $kmer_size --nb-threads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} #if $reference_source.reference_source_selector == 'history' --ref '$reference_source.ref' #else --ref '$__tool_directory__/data/flt3_exon14-15.fa' #end if --threshold $advanced.threshold --kmer-extend $advanced.kmer_extend --min-nb-duplication $advanced.min_nb_duplication --ignore-short-indels $advanced.ignore_short_indels --bloom-size $advanced.bloom_size --nb-hash $advanced.nb_hash --highest-fp-rate $advanced.highest_fp_rate $advanced.no_bloom_filter $advanced.no_subst_removal $advanced.low_mem #if $vcf_output.make_vcf == 'yes' --vcf $vcf_output.out_sequences #end if --out 'filt3r.results.json' ## --sequences is greedy (consumes every following token), so keep it last. --sequences #for $seq in $sequences '$seq' #end for

param.name: sequences
param.label: Sequence file(s)
param.help: Reads to analyse: FASTA or FASTQ, optionally gzipped. Select several files (e.g. R1 and R2) to analyse them together.

param.name: kmer_size
param.label: K-mer size (-k)
param.help: Length of the k-mers used. The FiLT3r paper used 12 with the FLT3 exon 14-15 reference; tune it for other references.

param.name: reference_source_selector
param.label: Reference sequence
param.help: Use the built-in FLT3 exon 14-15 reference or supply your own.

param.label: Reference sequence
param.help: Single-sequence FASTA. For genomic coordinates in the output, use a header of the form chr:start-end:strand (e.g. 13:28033760-28034429:-1).

param.label: Write duplication sequences into the VCF (--out-sequences)
Read k-mer match ratio (--threshold)
param.help: Fraction of a read's k-mers that must match the reference for the read to be fully analysed; all others are discarded.

param.label: Ignore indels up to this length (--ignore-short-indels)
Number of Bloom hash functions (--nb-hash)
param.help: 0 picks the optimal number for the false-positive rate (may not be optimal for speed).

param.label: Low-memory mode (--low-mem)
param.help: Store reads with duplications on disk so memory use is nearly independent of the number of breakpoints.

tool.help:
## What it does
FiLT3r detects and quantifies internal duplications in raw sequencing data using an alignment-free k-mer approach, so it is fast and memory-frugal. It was designed and benchmarked for detecting and quantifying FLT3 internal tandem duplications (FLT3-ITD) from capture and RNA-Seq data.
## Inputs
- **Sequence file(s)**: FASTA or FASTQ (optionally gzipped). Select several files to analyse them together (for example paired R1 and R2 reads). - **Reference**: the built-in FLT3 exon 14-15 sequence (default) or your own single-sequence FASTA. Give the reference a `chr:start-end:strand` header to get genomic coordinates in the output. - **K-mer size**: 12 works well with the FLT3 reference; tune it for others.
## Outputs
- **JSON report** with, per breakpoint, the duplication size, read support, region and wild-type coverage, MT/WT ratio and variant allele frequency (VAF), plus the duplicated sequence. - **VCF** (optional).
See the [FiLT3r documentation](https://gitlab.univ-lille.fr/filt3r/filt3r/-/blob/master/README.md) for the full description of every output field.
